name: Panzoom
when_to_use: 需要给一个「过大的生成内容」(大 SVG 架构/流程/电路图、超宽表/图、canvas)加平滑 pan + 滚轮/捏合 zoom + 边界约束,让它在固定视口里可浏览时用它。超高清 tiled 图(gigapixel/IIIF 渐进加载)用 openseadragon;普通图集 lightbox 用 photoswipe。
why_valuable: AI 生成的大 SVG/超宽渲染在固定视口里看不全,而手写 transform + 指针/滚轮/捏合手势 + 惯性 + inside/outside 约束是一大坨易错状态机;Panzoom 用 CSS transform 把它收敛成 Panzoom(elem, options) 一行,并暴露 zoom/pan/reset/getScale 等原生方法。

data_in.note: Panzoom 作用于「被平移缩放的目标元素」,该元素须放进一个尺寸确定、overflow:hidden 的父视口(父=视口,子=被拖动的画布)。关键:滚轮缩放不是默认绑定,须消费方在父视口上手动挂 wheel→pz.zoomWithWheel。data 只给内容与选项;真实消费中也可直接对已存在元素调用,不经 content 字段。

schema.content: string —— 要被平移缩放的内容(SVG/HTML 标记或 ),由 boot 放进目标元素;也可省略而由消费方对既有元素调用

options?._default: 省略时使用下列上游默认值(实测自 4.6.2 defaultOptions)
options?.step?: number —— 滚轮/捏合/zoomIn 每步缩放,默认 0.3
options?.contain?: 'inside' | 'outside' —— 边界约束模式,默认不约束
options?.animate?: boolean —— 过渡动画,默认 false(duration 默认 200ms)

spec.note: 父视口容器需显式像素高度(默认 400px)且 overflow:hidden;子内容可任意大,Panzoom 用 transform 在此视口内平移缩放。禁 vh/百分比高(铁律 3)。

example._why: 常被误解为默认支持滚轮缩放、且直接作用于父容器;minimal 钉死「目标元素 + 选项」且缩放范围/约束可控。

minimal.content: <svg width='1600' height='1200' viewBox='0 0 1600 1200'><!-- 大架构图 --></svg>

uplink.artifact: artifact 无上行;当前缩放/位移由 pz.getScale()/pz.getPan() 读取,消费 AI 如需回传自行导出。
uplink.widget: 消费 AI 在确认控件读取 pz.getScale()/pz.getPan() 后拼 sendPrompt;本条不焊接统一事件出口(panzoom 原生派发 panzoomchange/panzoomzoom CustomEvent 可供监听)。
uplink.codex: 消费 AI 读取 pz.getScale()/pz.getPan() 后自行调用 sendFollowUpMessage。

carriers.widget: supported:Panzoom 只对 shadow 内目标元素与父视口写 transform/style 并绑定它们自身的指针事件,无 head/body/document 逃逸
carriers.codex: supported:纯 ESM + CSS transform + 指针事件,无 worker/wasm 依赖

isolation.evidence: @panzoom/panzoom@4.6.2 +esm default export `lt as default` = Panzoom(elem:HTMLElement|SVGElement, options?:PanzoomGlobalOptions)=>PanzoomObject (dist/src/panzoom.d.ts). PanzoomObject methods zoomWithWheel(event,opts), zoomIn/zoomOut(opts), pan/zoom, reset(opts), getScale(), getPan(), setOptions(opts), destroy() (types.d.ts L215-395). Wheel zoom is NOT auto-bound — d.ts example (L383) shows elem.parentElement.addEventListener('wheel', panzoom.zoomWithWheel); boot follows this. defaultOptions verified in /tmp bundle: minScale:.125, maxScale:4, step:.3, startScale:1, cursor:'move', animate:false, disablePan:false, disableZoom:false, duration:200, overflow:'hidden', touchAction:'none'. Footprint: 0 document.head / createElement('style') / adoptedStyleSheets; 0 document.|ownerDocument.addEventListener in bundle — pointer/mouse/touch listeners are attached to the target element and its parent (bind target is the element var, uses pointer capture), NOT to document/window, and destroy() removes them. So Panzoom writes transform/transform-origin/touch-action on the target and touch-action/overflow on its parent, all inside the mounted subtree → shadow-safe with no global footprint.

en.when_to_use: Use it when you need to add smooth pan, wheel/pinch zoom, and boundary constraints to oversized generated content (large SVG architecture/flow/circuit diagrams, extra-wide tables/charts, or canvas) so it can be explored in a fixed viewport. For ultra-high-resolution tiled images (gigapixel/IIIF progressive loading) use openseadragon; for a regular image-gallery lightbox use photoswipe.
en.why_valuable: Large AI-generated SVG or extra-wide output does not fit in a fixed viewport, while hand-writing transform plus pointer/wheel/pinch gestures, inertia, and inside/outside constraints creates a large error-prone state machine. Panzoom reduces this to Panzoom(elem, options) using CSS transform and exposes native methods such as zoom/pan/reset/getScale.

data_in.note: Panzoom acts on the target element being panned and zoomed. That element must be inside a fixed-size parent viewport with overflow:hidden (parent=viewport, child=draggable canvas). Important: wheel zoom is not bound by default; the consumer must manually attach wheel -> pz.zoomWithWheel on the parent viewport. data contains only content and options; in real use it may also be called directly on an existing element without the content field.

schema.content: string -- Content to pan and zoom (SVG/HTML markup or ), placed in the target element by boot; may be omitted when the consumer calls Panzoom on an existing element

options?._default: When omitted, uses the following upstream defaults (verified from 4.6.2 defaultOptions)
options?.step?: number -- Scale increment for wheel/pinch/zoomIn; default 0.3
options?.animate?: boolean -- Transition animation; default false (duration defaults to 200ms)

spec.note: The parent viewport needs an explicit pixel height (default 400px) and overflow:hidden. Child content may be arbitrarily large; Panzoom uses transform to pan and zoom within this viewport. vh/percentage height is prohibited (Rule 3).
